meta.titleTemplate: Which Shipping Lines Carry Charcoal? | UN 1361 DG
meta.descriptionTemplate: Not every carrier ships Class {{unClass}} charcoal. The lines we book directly for declared UN 1361 dangerous goods, and the audit they require.

h1: Which shipping lines carry coconut charcoal?
heroSubtitle: Coconut charcoal is Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods, and not every carrier will take it. Here are the lines we book directly and what booking requires.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Ask about current carriers on WhatsApp
hero.ctaContactAria: Send an inquiry about shipping lines

intro.p1: This page sits under the {{brand}} Logistics pillar. Because charcoal is declared dangerous goods, carrier choice is a compliance decision, not just a freight one.

keyFacts.heading: Carriers at a glance
keyFacts.definition: How coconut charcoal is booked onto a vessel.

whyDg.h2: Why charcoal needs a DG-capable carrier
whyDg.body: Since the 2026 rules, UN 1361 must be carried as declared dangerous goods. A carrier has to be set up to accept Class {{unClass}} self-heating cargo, apply the SP 978 handling, and — increasingly — audit the factory it loads from. Lines that are not, simply decline the cargo.
whyDg.regulationLinkLabel: Why the 2026 rules require DG booking

carriers.acceptIntro: We book coconut charcoal directly with carriers that accept declared UN 1361 dangerous goods:
carriers.acceptEmpty: Contact us for the current list of carriers.
carriers.declineIntro: Several major lines have declined charcoal bookings. We name them so you can plan your routing:
carriers.declineNote: Carrier acceptance changes; we confirm the current line and schedule at booking.

audit.h2: Do you need a factory audit to book?
audit.body: Some carriers require a dangerous-goods or fire-safety audit of the producing factory before they will load its UN 1361 cargo. Where a carrier requires it, we hold that audit — the buyer does not have to arrange anything. It is part of why we can book the lines we do.

forwarder.h2: Can I use my own forwarder?
forwarder.body: Yes. Under EXW or {{incotermDefault}} you are free to nominate your own freight forwarder; we hand them a fully declared, document-complete UN 1361 container. Many buyers prefer this to consolidate their own ocean contracts.

items.a: We book coconut charcoal directly with carriers that accept declared UN 1361, Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods — currently {{carriersAudited}}. Acceptance changes over time, so we confirm the line and schedule at booking.

items.q: Do I need a factory audit to book?
items.a: Where a carrier requires a dangerous-goods or fire-safety audit of the producing factory, we hold it — the buyer does not arrange anything. It is one reason we can book the carriers we do for Class {{unClass}} charcoal.

items.q: Can I use my own freight forwarder?
items.a: Yes. Under EXW or {{incotermDefault}} you can nominate your own forwarder, and we hand them a fully declared, document-complete UN 1361 container ready to load.
